title: Eugenia virens
taxonomy: Eugenia virens is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This species is native to the Americas, with a wide distribution in Central and South America. The plant is commonly known by its local names, such as the 'White Guava' or 'Maracujá Branco'. Eugenia virens is classified within the genus Eugenia, which comprises over 700 species of flowering plants. The Myrtaceae family, to which Eugenia virens belongs, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Eugenia virens is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, including treating gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and fruits are used in traditional medicine, with the leaves being used to treat fever and the fruits being used to treat digestive problems. Eugenia virens is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties make it useful for treating wounds and preventing infections.

The fruit is high in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation.
